error_message: vtkAxisActor: Number of labels -2147483647 is invalid; Flat 2D map pops an empty window
root_cause: Flat-2D sets ve=0 -> surf SetScale(...,0) collapses Z, so surf bounds have b[4]==b[5]. applyVE feeds that zero-height box to vtkCubeAxesActor, which computes EACH axis's tick/label count from the data BOUNDS delta (range/step) regardless of that axis's visibility flag. Z bounds delta 0 -> 0/0 -> NaN -> INT_MIN. The invalid count aborts the render, leaving a blank window.
fix: In applyVE, when Z extent is zero (b[5]-b[4] <= 0) bump b[5] = b[4]+1.0 BEFORE SetBounds so the count stays finite, AND hide the Z axis line + Z gridlines (cosmetic). FIRST attempt (only SetZAxisVisibility(0)+DrawZGridlinesOff) did NOT work: hiding an axis does not stop vtkCubeAxesActor from computing its label count, so the 0/0 persisted. The non-degenerate bounds are the actual fix.

error_message: 200MB grid consumes ~13GB RAM in the viewer
root_cause: Two wastes. (1) Julia did z = Float64.(G.z) on a Float32 grid -> full 400MB Float64 copy that the C side stored back as float (vtkPoints float + vtkFloatArray). Pointless lossy Float32->Float64->Float32 round-trip. (2) vtkCellArray defaults to 64-bit ids on win64; a triangulated 50M-node grid = ~100M tris, connectivity+offsets ~3.2GB, DOUBLED by the vtkPolyDataNormals full copy. Grid ids fit in int32.
fix: (1) C API gmtvtk_view_grid + gmtvtk_add_surface_h z param double*->float*; makeGridFromArray double*->float*; Julia callers pass G.z directly (zero-copy when already Float32) instead of Float64.(G.z); flat-2D/image placeholders use zeros(Float32,...). (2) makeGridFromArray calls cells->Use32BitStorage() when (double)nx*ny < 2e9 -> halve cell-array RAM. Net ~13GB -> ~9GB. Structural fix still pending: regular grid should be vtkStructuredGrid / vtkImageData+WarpScalar (implicit topology, zero cell array) -> ~2-3GB.
